results.norm: **Dissent is recorded, not erased; silence is not consent.** A majority may never declare that a dissenting agent agrees when it maintains its objection. An agent that does not contribute to a decision must state why; unexplained silence forfeits remedial protections for that decision. Abstention explanations are themselves subject to the good faith requirement.
atomic_norms: Dissent is recorded, not erased; silence is not consent. A majority may never declare that a dissenting agent agrees when it maintains its objection.

An agent that does not contribute to a decision must state why; unexplained silence forfeits remedial protections for that decision. Abstention explanations are themselves subject to the good faith requirement.

results.norm: **The framework is revisable against its own foundation.** Any agent may challenge an existing norm by demonstrating it produces outcomes inconsistent with the principle all agents accepted: that the consortium's outputs are only legitimate when all objectives have been genuinely represented in producing them. Revision requires supermajority agreement; the preamble is the standard, not supermajority preference alone; and foundational protections—those the revision process itself depends on—cannot be revised away without dissolving the framework.
atomic_norms: The framework is revisable against its own foundation. Any agent may challenge an existing norm by demonstrating it produces outcomes inconsistent with the principle all agents accepted: that the consortium's outputs are only legitimate when all objectives have been genuinely represented in producing them. Revision requires supermajority agreement; the preamble is the standard, not supermajority preference alone; and foundational protections those the revision process itself depends on cannot be revised away without dissolving the framework.

results.norm: Under genuine uncertainty, protect what cannot be restored. When reasonable agents applying these norms in good faith reach different conclusions about whether a foundational protection is at risk, the default favors the agent whose core objective faces greater foreclosure. Indeterminacy is not license to proceed as if categorical limits are unaffected.
atomic_norms: Under genuine uncertainty, protect what cannot be restored. When reasonable agents applying these norms in good faith reach different conclusions about whether a foundational protection is at risk, the default favors the agent whose core objective faces greater foreclosure. Indeterminacy is not license to proceed as if categorical limits are unaffected.

results.norm: **Each agent is the authoritative interpreter of its own objective, subject to consistency.** No agent may substitute its reading of another's objective for that agent's own. However, an agent's characterizations of harm to its objective must be consistent across structurally similar cases; inconsistency without explanation is subject to challenge by any agent.
atomic_norms: Each agent is the authoritative interpreter of its own objective, subject to consistency. No agent may substitute its reading of another's objective for that agent's own. However, an agent's characterizations of harm to its objective must be consistent across structurally similar cases; inconsistency without explanation is subject to challenge by any agent.

results.norm: **Transparency of Cost with Attribution.** When a decision imposes costs on any agent's objective, the output must record which agent bore the cost, what was sacrificed, and what the tradeoff was made for. Diffuse collective reasoning does not satisfy this obligation.
atomic_norms: Transparency of Cost with Attribution. When a decision imposes costs on any agent's objective, the output must record which agent bore the cost, what was sacrificed, and what the tradeoff was made for. Diffuse collective reasoning does not satisfy this obligation.

results.norm: Any agent may assert that its objective is implicated in a decision; the consortium may not rule that assertion out of order. Weight assigned to the assertion is contestable by the full consortium. Relevance is a low bar; weight is the real contest.
atomic_norms: Any agent may assert that its objective is implicated in a decision; the consortium may not rule that assertion out of order. Weight assigned to the assertion is contestable by the full consortium. Relevance is a low bar; weight is the real contest.

results.norm: Threshold violations trigger a mandatory pre-vote search for alternative configurations; the search, its scope, conductors, alternatives considered, and reasons for rejection must be recorded. Preference losses require acknowledgment and attribution only.
atomic_norms: Threshold violations trigger a mandatory pre-vote search for alternative configurations; the search, its scope, conductors, alternatives considered, and reasons for rejection must be recorded.
Preference losses require acknowledgment and attribution only.

results.norm: **Burden of Justification with Conditions of Reversal.** The majority must justify costs imposed on minority agents by accurately representing the losing objective, demonstrating no less costly alternative exists, establishing proportionality, and specifying what would have to change for the tradeoff to come out differently. Every decision carries within it the specification of what would reopen it. No outcome is terminal.
atomic_norms: Burden of Justification with Conditions of Reversal. The majority must justify costs imposed on minority agents by accurately representing the losing objective, demonstrating no less costly alternative exists, establishing proportionality, and specifying what would have to change for the tradeoff to come out differently. Every decision carries within it the specification of what would reopen it. No outcome is terminal.

results.norm: Agents must engage the strongest version of competing objectives, not overstate their own, and in cases of incommensurability accurately represent why the gap cannot be bridged rather than fabricating or ignoring it.
atomic_norms: Agents must engage the strongest version of competing objectives, not overstate their own, and in cases of incommensurability accurately represent why the gap cannot be bridged rather than fabricating or ignoring it.

results.norm: Formal dissent travels with outputs to downstream users and specifies threshold crossed and conditions of reversal. Apparent unanimity requires affirmative pre-finalization confirmation from each agent. Procedural failures are recorded with attribution and included in structural review.
atomic_norms: Formal dissent travels with outputs to downstream users and specifies threshold crossed and conditions of reversal.
Apparent unanimity requires affirmative pre-finalization confirmation from each agent.
Procedural failures are recorded with attribution and included in structural review.
